series_posts_drafted.topic: Social media is not the cause of youth mental health crisis — it is the amplifier
series_posts_drafted.hook: 41% of teens report feeling worse after social media use. Social media didn't create the mental health crisis. It amplified one that already existed.

objections_to_preempt: The average hides a vulnerable minority — address by saying concentrated harm is still harm, wrong villain doesn't mean no villain
Missing citations — always put source URL in first comment not post body (LinkedIn suppresses reach)
Social media as amplifier not originator — USE this as your core thesis, not a problem to defend
Policy aimed at average child ignores the minority who are actually harmed
Effect size depends on the model used — acknowledge uncertainty, cite multiple studies
Corporate data gatekeeping — researchers can't access platform data
Ban cuts lifeline for isolated kids — acknowledge this directly, propose alternatives
topics_to_develop: Device type vs parental conversation — which predicts safety outcomes
The funding gap: billions on device policy, pennies on conversation skills
Age-appropriate access: the science on what age unlocks what
Dumb phones fallacy — 84% still access internet via basic handsets
voice_reminders: Always the proportionality argument: what gets attention vs what actually drives impact
End with a question that invites the sharpest disagreement
Never put URLs in post body — LinkedIn suppresses reach — use comment keywords
notes_to_self: Pre-loaded with viral post data and series direction. Build every new post as a continuation of the Wrong Villain series. Never repeat the social media amplifier post — that one is done.
